san-jose.title: San José City Council — March 25, 2026
san-jose.summary: This San José City Council meeting on March 25, 2026 provided information about how residents can watch the meeting through multiple channels including cable TV, the city website, YouTube, phone, and Zoom. The meeting agenda focused on ensuring accessibility, with live translations available in over 50 languages for residents who need them.
keyTopics: Multiple ways to watch the meeting (cable, online, phone, Zoom)
Live translations available in 50+ languages
Meeting accessibility for all residents
No public comment period at this session

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — March 25, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The Mountain View City Council's zoning hearing that was supposed to happen on March 25, 2026 has been canceled. The next zoning hearing will take place on April 8, 2026 instead. If you had plans to attend or present something at the March meeting, you'll need to wait until the April date.
keyTopics: March 25 zoning hearing cancelled
Rescheduled to April 8, 2026
Administrative zoning decisions postponed

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — March 25, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Housing and Human Services Commission met to review applications for federal grant money that helps pay for housing and community development projects in Sunnyvale. The commission approved meeting notes from their previous month and heard presentations from organizations requesting funding.
keyTopics: Federal housing grant applications being reviewed
Community development funding decisions
February meeting minutes approval
Local organizations seeking housing/services funding

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — March 27, 2026
cupertino.summary: The City Council focused on helping local businesses grow and succeed. They discussed a program to help businesses that may have missed renewing their licenses, plus plans to encourage shopping at local stores through community events and special campaigns. The city is also looking at ways to support small businesses and startups through incentives and partnerships.
keyTopics: Business License Amnesty Program for shopping centers
Summer of Celebrations Shop Local campaign
Economic incentives for retail and startups
Public-Private partnerships for city events
Business outreach and participation programs

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — March 24, 2026
santa-clara.summary: The Santa Clara City Council held a meeting on March 24, 2026 to address several items, including approving a plan to remove weeds around the city as part of the county's weed abatement program. The council also invited residents to give feedback on how the city plans to spend federal housing grants for the next fiscal year.
keyTopics: Weed removal program - city will abate nuisances caused by overgrown weeds
Federal housing grants - public comment period on how HUD funds will be used in 2026/27
Multiple items postponed or reconsidered from the agenda

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — March 23, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The Palo Alto City Council met on March 23, 2026 to approve several routine items, including a proclamation celebrating Women's History Month. The council also agreed to let the American Red Cross use city facilities during emergencies and approved a three-year contract with a software company to manage the city's email security system.
keyTopics: Women's History Month proclamation
American Red Cross disaster relief agreement for city facilities
Email security software contract ($93,383/year for 3 years)
